sprint: Sprint 30 -- V0.27.4 Bug Fixes (sync UPDATE propagation + calibration.py PYTHONPATH)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #3 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.4 branches FROM sprint/sprint29-bugfixes-V0.27.3 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. Two stories: US-315 fixes B-065 (sync client INSERT-only / 6 of 6 reproducible) + US-316 fixes I-020 (calibration.py module-not-found 'src' on local Windows invocation -- third layer of I-018 that V0.27.3 US-312 didn't catch).

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.3 baseline (V0.27.3 sprint-tip @ 7de5400). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Fix two carryforward bugs from V0.27.3 IRL validation. (1) B-065 sync UPDATE propagation gap -- 6 of 6 drains (10-15) show Pi-side close-event UPDATE never propagates to server; PM code-level confirmation: src/pi/sync/client.py uses last_synced_id high-water cursor; once a row INSERTs, no mechanism re-pushes UPDATEs. (2) I-020 calibration.py ModuleNotFoundError 'src' -- third layer of I-018 not caught by V0.27.3 US-312 IRL validation; CIO Windows-local invocation crashes at import line 58. PM testing-discipline gap: V0.27.3 US-312 was validated server-side only; needed CIO-shell validation too.
EXECUTION ORDER (parallel): TIER 1 (P1, parallel) -- US-315 sync UPDATE propagation + US-316 calibration.py PYTHONPATH bootstrap. Independent stories; no inter-dependency.

THEME: 'Server mirror catches up + CLI works from CIO shell.' V0.27 chain has been writing fine on Pi but server-side analytics mirror has been silently lagging on UPDATEs since V0.27.2 deploy (US-315). And V0.27.3 US-312 closed I-018 layers 1+2 but missed layer 3 (PYTHONPATH on local invocation; US-316).

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28-29 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) BL-010 + verify-table-names rule applied. (d) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM reproduced INSERT-only cursor empirically at code level (src/pi/sync/client.py:433-434) AND reproduced calibration.py crash from CIO's exact invocation path -- story scopes reflect verified premises. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran rg src/pi/sync/ during grooming + verified line 58 import in src/server/analytics/calibration.py; baked findings into scope.filesToTouch. (f) CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge: ALL implementation commits land on sprint/sprint30-bugfixes-V0.27.4; main waits for whole V0.27 chain fully functional working. (g) NEW STANDING RULE post-I-020: when validating CLI fixes, the IRL gate must include running from CIO's actual shell (Windows, no env vars set), not just the path PM knows works.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-10 server-side sync gap correction note + Spool 2026-05-10 Drain Test 15 validation report (6/6 reproduction) + PM 2026-05-10 Drain Test 15 forensics + PM 2026-05-10 code-level audit of src/pi/sync/client.py (US-315). CIO 2026-05-10 calibration.py crash from local Windows shell (US-316).

POST-SPRINT-30 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO completes B-063 fuse-box wiring (gates Drive 11+ for V0.27.2/3/4 IRL validation). (2) Drive 11+ post-fuse-box first clean car-coupled drive validates sync UPDATE propagation IRL via drive_summary metadata + drive_counter advance. (3) Once V0.27 chain fully clean, CIO directs chain merge to main as new fully-functional-working stable.

stories.title: sync-client UPDATE propagation for delta-tables (B-065)
stories.intent: B-065: Pi-side sync client (src/pi/sync/client.py) uses sync_log.getHighWaterMark / getDeltaRows with WHERE id > last_synced_id cursor logic. Once a row INSERTs and the cursor advances past its id, no mechanism re-pushes UPDATEs to that row. Empirical evidence: 6 of 6 drains (10-15) show Pi-side close-event UPDATE not propagated to server -- INSERT propagates within 0-5 seconds, UPDATE never propagates. PM 2026-05-10 pre-flight `rg UPDATE.*SET src/pi/` enumerated UPDATE-issuing tables: battery_health_log + drive_summary are the ONLY DELTA_SYNC_TABLES that issue UPDATEs from Pi (statistics + dtc_log + connection_log + realtime_data + ai_recommendations + alert_log + calibration_sessions are INSERT-only on Pi side, so B-065 doesn't affect them). drive_counter is NOT in IN_SCOPE_TABLES (separate mechanism; V0.27.3 US-314 watch-item evaluates whether US-314 needs follow-on fix or is independently working). V0.27.4 US-315 narrows fix to 2 tables: battery_health_log + drive_summary. Fix shape: track per-table updated_at cursor alongside last_synced_id; sync loop pushes rows where (id > last_synced_id) OR (updated_at > last_synced_updated_at); server-side handler accepts UPSERT semantics for UPDATE-able fields per Spool's Spec 3 _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE.

filesToTouch: src/pi/sync/client.py (UPDATE -- _pushTable / _pushDelta logic gains UPDATE propagation: in addition to id > last_synced_id INSERT delta, query rows where updated_at > last_synced_updated_at + push them via UPSERT semantics; advance last_synced_updated_at on success)

src/pi/data/sync_log.py (UPDATE -- sync_log schema gains last_synced_updated_at column; getHighWaterMark / updateHighWaterMark API gains updated_at handling; per-table SUPPORTS_UPDATE_SYNC config dict identifies which tables (battery_health_log + drive_counter + drive_summary))
src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (UPDATE -- ensure battery_health_log + drive_counter + drive_summary have updated_at columns; idempotent ALTER TABLE for existing DBs OR new migration helper)

src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE -- POST handler accepts UPSERT for the UPDATE-syncable tables; respect _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE per Spool's Spec 3 race semantics on drive_summary; battery_health_log close-event fields populate cleanly without clobbering INSERT-side fields)

tests/pi/sync/test_update_propagation.py (NEW -- integration test: Pi INSERTs row -> sync pushes -> Pi UPDATEs same row -> sync re-pushes -> server reflects UPDATE within 60s. Three table coverage: battery_health_log close-event, drive_counter advance, drive_summary metadata fill. Would FAIL pre-fix because UPDATE never propagates.)

tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py (V0.27.3 US-314 test; identifies whether US-314 fix is independent of B-065 or merges into this story)

stories.title: calibration.py local-invocation PYTHONPATH bootstrap (I-020)
stories.intent: I-020: V0.27.3 US-312 closed I-018 layers 1 (types.py shadow rename) + 2 (missing baselines table migration). CIO 2026-05-10 hit a THIRD layer when running locally on Windows: `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` crashes at line 58 with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'`. The script imports `from src.server.db.models import ...` which requires `src` to be on sys.path as a top-level package. PM validated US-312 server-side only (with PYTHONPATH set explicitly); never tested CIO's local Windows invocation. V0.27.4 adds a self-bootstrapping sys.path block at the top of calibration.py so the script works from any working directory regardless of env vars. Plus a subprocess-invocation regression test that exercises the entry point in CIO's exact usage context.

filesToTouch: src/server/analytics/calibration.py (UPDATE -- add sys.path bootstrap block at top of file, BEFORE the `from src.server.db.models import` line: `import sys; from pathlib import Path; sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent))`. Standard idiom for Python CLI scripts in src/-layout repos. Idempotent -- inserting a path that's already there is harmless)

tests/server/test_calibration_cli_local_invocation.py (NEW -- subprocess.run-based test: invoke calibration.py via `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` from a fresh subprocess with NO PYTHONPATH set, NO repo-root cwd; assert exit-0 OR clean error message about missing source data. Would FAIL pre-fix on ModuleNotFoundError at import time.)
filesToRead: offices/pm/issues/I-020-calibration-py-module-not-found-src.md (full bug spec + CIO crash trace + recommended fix)
tests/server/test_calibration_cli_integration.py (V0.27.3 US-312 server-side validation test; identifies why local-invocation case wasn't covered)

groundingRefs.value: CIO 2026-05-10 crash from local Windows shell: `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` -> Traceback at line 58 -> `from src.server.db.models import Baseline, DriveStatistic, DriveSummary` -> ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'

PM testing-discipline gap (V0.27.3 US-312): IRL validation ran on chi-srv-01 with PYTHONPATH=/mnt/projects/O/OBD2v2 explicit. CIO's actual usage = local Windows shell, no env vars. The validation path didn't exercise CIO's reality.
Standard Python idiom: src/-layout CLI scripts add `sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent))` to bootstrap. Idempotent + tolerates being run from any cwd
groundingRefs.source: Python community standard for src/-layout repos
